title: Preventing unauthorized account access based on location and time
abstract: The subject matter of this specification can be implemented in, among other things, a method that includes storing, in a collaboration platform, a first geographic location and a first time of a first authentication request for an account at the collaboration platform responsive to successful authentication of the first authentication request. The method includes receiving a second authentication request for the account at the collaboration platform. The method includes identifying a second geographic location and a second time of the second authentication request. The method includes providing access to the account responsive to a determination that a difference in time between the first time and the second time is large enough that a user of the account is able to travel a difference in distance between the first geographic location and the second geographic location within the difference in time.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method, comprising: storing, by at least one processing device in a collaboration platform, a first geographic location and a first time of a first authentication request for an account at the collaboration platform responsive to successful authentication of the first authentication request; receiving, by the processing device, a second authentication request for the account at the collaboration platform; identifying, by the processing device, a second geographic location and a second time of the second authentication request; determining whether a difference in time between the first time and the second time is large enough that a user of the account is able to travel a difference in distance between the first geographic location and the second geographic location within the difference in time; and providing access to the account responsive to a determination that the difference in time is large enough and to successful authentication of the second authentication request. 2. The method of claim 1, further comprising: storing, by the processing device in the collaboration platform, first client device information of the first authentication request responsive to the successful authentication of the first authentication request; identifying, by the processing device, second client device information of the second authentication request; and determining that the second client device information is different than the first client device information, wherein the determination that the difference in time is large enough is responsive to the determination that the second client device information is different than the first client device information. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the first client device information comprises a first network address of a first client device that sent the first authentication request to the collaboration platform, and wherein the second client device information comprises a second 

 network address of a second client device that sent the second authentication request to the collaboration platform. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein the first client device information comprises a first browser fingerprint of a first browser at the first client device that sent the first authentication request to the collaboration platform, and wherein the second client device information comprises a second browser fingerprint of a second browser at the second client device that sent the second authentication request to the collaboration platform. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein determining whether the difference in time is large enough to travel the difference in distance comprises comparing the difference in time and the difference in distance to a plurality of thresholds corresponding to a plurality of ranges of time and distance. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the thresholds represent speeds of the user for the ranges of time and distance. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein the speeds increase for the ranges of time and distance as the ranges of time and distance get further from the first time and the first geographic location. 8. A non-transitory computer-readable medium including instructions stored therein that, in response to execution by at least one processing device, cause the processing device to perform or control performance of operations that comprise: store, by the processing device in a collaboration platform, a first geographic location and a first time of a first authentication request for an account at the collaboration platform responsive to successful authentication of the first authentication request; receive, by the processing device, a second authentication request for the account at the collaboration platform; identify, by the processing device, a second geographic location and a second time of the second authentication request; determine whether a difference in time between the first time and the second time is large enough that a user of the account is able to travel a difference in distance between the 

 first geographic location and the second geographic location within the difference in time; and provide access to the account responsive to a determination that the difference in time is large enough and to successful authentication of the second authentication request. 9. The computer-readable medium of claim 8, wherein the instructions are further executable to cause the processing to perform or control performance of operations that comprise: store, by the processing device in the collaboration platform, first client device information of the first authentication request responsive to the successful authentication of the first authentication request; identify, by the processing device, second client device information of the second authentication request; and determine that the second client device information is different than the first client device information, wherein the determination that the difference in time is large enough is responsive to the determination that the second client device information is different than the first client device information. 10. The computer-readable medium of claim 9, wherein the first client device information comprises a first network address of a first client device that sent the first authentication request to the collaboration platform, and wherein the second client device information comprises a second network address of a second client device that sent the second authentication request to the collaboration platform. 11. The computer-readable medium of claim 10, wherein the first client device information comprises a first browser fingerprint of a first browser at the first client device that sent the first authentication request to the collaboration platform, and wherein the second client device information comprises a second browser fingerprint of a second browser at the second client device that sent the second authentication request to the collaboration platform. 12. The computer-readable medium of claim 8, wherein to determine whether the difference in time is large enough to travel the difference in distance, the instructions are 

 further executable to cause the processing device to perform or control performance of operations that comprise: compare the difference in time and the difference in distance to a plurality of thresholds corresponding to a plurality of ranges of time and distance. 13. The computer-readable medium of claim 12, wherein the thresholds represent speeds of the user for the ranges of time and distance. 14. The computer-readable medium of claim 13, wherein the speeds increase for the ranges of time and distance as the ranges of time and distance get further from the first time and the first geographic location. 15. A system, comprising: at least one memory that stores instructions; and at least one processing device to execute the instructions to: store, in a collaboration platform, a first geographic location and a first time of a first authentication request for an account at the collaboration platform responsive to successful authentication of the first authentication request; receive a second authentication request for the account at the collaboration platform; identify a second geographic location and a second time of the second authentication request; determine whether a difference in time between the first time and the second time is large enough that a user of the account is able to travel a difference in distance between the first geographic location and the second geographic location within the difference in time; and provide access to the account responsive to a determination that the difference in time is large enough and to successful authentication of the second authentication request. 16. The system of claim 15, wherein the processing device is further to execute the instructions to: store, by the processing device in the collaboration platform, first client device information of the first authentication request responsive to the successful authentication of 

 the first authentication request; identify, by the processing device, second client device information of the second authentication request; and determine that the second client device information is different than the first client device information, wherein the determination that the difference in time is large enough is responsive to the determination that the second client device information is different than the first client device information. 17. The system of claim 16, wherein the first client device information comprises a first network address of a first client device that sent the first authentication request to the collaboration platform, and wherein the second client device information comprises a second network address of a second client device that sent the second authentication request to the collaboration platform. 18. The system of claim 17, wherein the first client device information comprises a first browser fingerprint of a first browser at the first client device that sent the first authentication request to the collaboration platform, and wherein the second client device information comprises a second browser fingerprint of a second browser at the second client device that sent the second authentication request to the collaboration platform. 19. The system of claim 15, wherein to determine whether the difference in time is large enough to travel the difference in distance, the processing device is further to execute the instructions to compare the difference in time and the difference in distance to a plurality of thresholds corresponding to a plurality of ranges of time and distance. 20. The system of claim 19, wherein the thresholds represent speeds of the user for the ranges of time and distance, and wherein the speeds increase for the ranges of time and distance as the ranges of time and distance get further from the first time and the first geographic location.
